steps.title: Factor the equation
steps.content:
Move the right side to the left and factor out $x-a$.
$$(x-a)-(x-a)(x-18)=0$$
$$(x-a)(1-(x-18))=0$$
$$(x-a)(19-x)=0$$

steps.title: List the possible solutions
steps.content:
The factored equation is true when either factor is $0$.
$$x-a=0\quad\text{or}\quad 19-x=0$$
So the solutions are $x=a$ and $x=19$.

steps.title: Choose the valid statements
steps.content: Statement I and statement III are solutions. Statement II would use $x=18$, which does not make either factor $0$ because $a>19$. The answer is B.
